notes: Synthetic DSN, email, payload_json, customer/store keys, and row-object arrays were blocked.
Synthetic generic token field, URL query token, and personal-name free text were accepted.
Synthetic benign text containing the substring secret was blocked as a false positive.

findings.fact: read_json_path accepts any filesystem path that is_file() and below 5 MB, then reads it before any privacy scan or repository/sensitive-path guard.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:389-397
temp probe: absolute external JSON accepted
temp probe: traversal path accepted
temp probe: synthetic .env filename accepted
temp probe: synthetic .local_secrets path accepted
temp probe: synthetic credentials.json accepted
findings.consequence: The tool can read prohibited or sensitive paths if invoked with those paths, even when it does not print the original file content.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Create a synthetic valid JSON phase file in %TEMP% named .env or credentials.json and pass it as --phase-json; the script emits a candidate.
findings.required_correction: Reject absolute external paths, traversal, symlink escapes, .env, .env.*, .local_secrets, credentials-like filenames, data/, evidence/, and any path outside the allowed repo scope before opening the file.

findings.fact: draft and validate accept semantically invalid candidates because numeric counts, booleans, dates, hash format, optional collection types, and cross-field count consistency are not enforced.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:331-343
scripts/sz_load_observation.py:359-383
scripts/sz_load_observation.py:435-465
temp probe: input_rows as text accepted
temp probe: accepted_rows negative accepted
temp probe: accepted_rows greater than input_rows accepted
temp probe: loader_executed and db_write_executed as strings accepted
temp probe: short, non-hex, null, and empty input_file_sha256 accepted
findings.consequence: The script can produce or validate a candidate that matches field presence but violates the observation contract's technical meaning.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Pass SHAPE B with input_rows='100', accepted_rows=-1, or input_file_sha256='abc'; draft exits 0 and emits UNREVIEWED.
findings.required_correction: Add schema validation for all required and optional fields, including nonnegative integer counts, count relationships, booleans, ISO dates, SHA-256 hex length, list element types, and nullability rules.

findings.fact: The script accepts source, effective_week_start, and operation_type from CLI and ignores contradictory values present in SHAPE A or SHAPE B phase JSON.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:406-429
temp probe: phase source RUTA_RUTERO with CLI source KPIONE2 emitted source KPIONE2
temp probe: phase week 2026-06-08 with CLI week 2026-06-15 emitted 2026-06-15
temp probe: phase operation DRY_RUN with CLI APPLY emitted APPLY
findings.consequence: Evidence from one source, week, or operation can be labeled as another, which is a material traceability failure before ledger use.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Place source='RUTA_RUTERO' in the phase JSON but invoke draft with --source KPIONE2; the emitted candidate uses KPIONE2 without contradiction.
findings.required_correction: If phase JSON contains source, effective_week_start, operation_type, or file hash fields, require exact agreement with CLI arguments or fail closed with a JSON error.

findings.fact: observation_id is deterministic but not a reliable unique identity when input_file_sha256 is null, empty, malformed, or when the same source/week/operation/file is observed repeatedly.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:305-308
scripts/sz_load_observation.py:361
temp probe: null hash and empty hash produced the same ID
temp probe: same file, source, week, operation with different recorded_at produced the same ID
temp probe: malformed hash values were accepted
findings.consequence: The future ledger could conflate distinct observations or depend on invalid file identity values.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Draft two candidates with identical source/week/operation and missing hash; both receive the same LOADOBS identifier.
findings.required_correction: Require a valid SHA-256 input hash for ledger-suitable candidates and define whether observation_id is an idempotency key or event identity; include an explicit attempt/run discriminator if event uniqueness is required.

findings.fact: Shape detection and extraction accept partial or accidental SHAPE B objects and silently choose canonical fields over contradictory aliases.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:81-86
scripts/sz_load_observation.py:314-343
temp probe: object with only input_rows accepted
temp probe: object with only schema_signature accepted
temp probe: canonical db_write_executed=true and alias writes_executed=false emitted db_write_executed=true
findings.consequence: A non-load JSON object or contradictory dry-run aggregate can become a candidate with many null technical fields or silently selected values.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Pass {'schema_signature':'not really a load result'} as phase JSON; draft exits 0 and emits a candidate with null metrics.
findings.required_correction: Require a minimal complete shape signature, reject canonical/alias contradictions, and fail when required technical fields are absent.

findings.fact: Privacy checks are substring and key heuristics; they catch several forbidden classes but miss generic token-like fields, URL query secrets, and personal-name free text while also blocking benign text containing the substring secret.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:92-104
scripts/sz_load_observation.py:123-152
temp probe: service_token-style synthetic field accepted
temp probe: URL query token accepted
temp probe: synthetic personal name in notes accepted and emitted
temp probe: synthetic personal name in anomaly_reason accepted and emitted
temp probe: benign text with secret substring rejected
findings.consequence: The output can contain private free text, and safe benign text can be rejected unpredictably.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Invoke draft with --notes containing a synthetic personal name; the output includes the note.
findings.required_correction: Constrain free-text fields, broaden credential/token detection, sanitize or reject URL query strings, and define a documented privacy allowlist rather than raw substring matching.

findings.fact: Label rules enforce presence for some labels but allow several contradictory operational states.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:240-299
temp probe: CLEAN in PREFLIGHT with loader_executed=true accepted
temp probe: CLEAN in POST_LOAD_VALIDATION without db_write_executed accepted
temp probe: APPLY with loader_executed=false and UNREVIEWED accepted
temp probe: LOAD_FAILURE with post_load_validation_status OK accepted
temp probe: POST_LOAD_REGRESSION without db_write_executed accepted
findings.consequence: Labels do not guarantee coherent source-check, dry-run, apply, and post-validation semantics.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Invoke draft with label LOAD_FAILURE, loader_executed=true, post_load_validation_status=OK, reason, and evidence; the script emits the label.
findings.required_correction: Define and enforce operation-specific invariants for SOURCE_CHECK, PREFLIGHT, DRY_RUN, APPLY, POST_LOAD_VALIDATION, ROLLBACK, and each label.

findings.fact: Evidence refs are syntax-validated but existence and bounded length are not checked.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:107-110
scripts/sz_load_observation.py:211-234
temp probe: syntactically valid nonexistent commit accepted
temp probe: syntactically valid nonexistent phase accepted
temp probe: syntactically valid nonexistent research path accepted
temp probe: very long research ref accepted
findings.consequence: The candidate can cite evidence that does not exist. This is a limitation rather than a contract breach if existence is intentionally out of scope.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Pass --evidence-ref research/DOES_NOT_EXIST.json with an ANOMALOUS label; draft accepts the ref.
findings.required_correction: Either document evidence_refs as syntax-only or add optional existence checks and a maximum length.

findings.fact: argparse errors occur before the ObsError JSON wrapper and can emit usage text to stderr with no JSON stdout.
evidence: scripts/sz_load_observation.py:510-520
temp probe: draft with missing required arguments returned argparse usage on stderr
temp probe: unknown subcommand returned argparse usage on stderr
findings.consequence: The skill guarantee of JSON-only output is not true for malformed CLI invocations.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Call python scripts/sz_load_observation.py draft without required options; argparse emits usage text instead of a JSON error object.
findings.required_correction: Wrap argument parsing or subclass ArgumentParser.error to emit normalized JSON errors.

commands_run: temp-only Python harness importing scripts/sz_load_observation.py and creating synthetic fixtures in %TEMP%
